description: This stacked bar chart displays measured yield strength (red bars) and calculated contributions of different strengthening mechanisms to yield strength for four Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel samples. The four samples are M1-SS (Ti/V-free M1 steel, solution treated without aging), M1-30 (M1 steel aged at 550 °C for 30 h), M2-SS (Ti/V-modified M2 steel, solution treated without aging), and M2-30 (M2 steel aged at 550 °C for 30 h). The y-axis represents strength in MPa. Strengthening contribution components include lattice friction strength σ₀ (blue), grain boundary strengthening σ_G (purple), Orowan strengthening σ_Or from (Ti,V)C precipitates (green), and shear strengthening σ_S from κ-carbides (yellow). Measured yield strength values are ~430 MPa for M1-SS, ~860 MPa for M1-30, ~650 MPa for M2-SS, and ~890 MPa for M2-30. Aging significantly improves yield strength for both steels; Ti/V addition introduces (Ti,V)C precipitates that provide Orowan strengthening, compensating for reduced κ-carbide shear strengthening to enhance overall yield strength, supporting the proposed Ti/V microalloying strategy for mechanical property optimization.
